title: Software Engineer I
description:

****Title And Summary****

 Experiences

**Corporate Security Responsibility**
 All Activities Involving Access To Mastercard Assets, Information, And Networks Comes With An Inherent Risk To The Organization And, Therefore, It Is Expected That Every Person Working For, Or On Behalf Of, Mastercard Is Responsible For Information Security And Must:

* Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

title: Software Engineer
description:
**About Shinobi** 




 Shinobi security is an applied AI company that designs and develops autonomous offensive technologies for the world’s leading businesses and governments. Our teams in
 
 US, UK and Dubai build autonomous agents that find and exploit real vulnerabilities the way an attacker would, and we run them safely against live customer environments. The interesting part is not just "make the agents capable," it is "give an autonomous agent real credentials and real network access while making sure it only ever does what it's supposed to."
 




  






 We are small and fast, and we like it that way. Engineers here own systems end to end and get to talk directly to the customers running their software, which means you see the impact of what you build quickly.
 




  





**The Role** 




  






 This is a true founding engineering role. You will own a meaningful slice of the platform from architecture through production and the customer conversations that come out of it. You won't be handed a narrow ticket queue. You'll be handed real problems, sometimes ambiguous ones, and given the room and support to figure them out. We care more about how you think and how fast you learn than about whether you've seen this exact stack before.
 




  






 Concretely, the person in this role:
 




  




* Designs and operates the platform layer that runs our pentesting agents: orchestration, multi\\-tenant isolation, scoped and short\\-lived credential vending, and the transparent traffic interception that lets us observe and constrain what agents do on the wire.
* Builds the infrastructure underneath all of it on AWS (and increasingly on\\-prem / Kubernetes for customers who require it), including networking, data stores, and the auth layer that gates everything.
* Helps keep our compliance and security posture solid, including the controls that get us and keep us SOC 2 compliant.
* Handles real customer issues directly. When a customer hits a problem, you are the one who reproduces it, traces it through the platform, fixes the root cause, and closes the loop with them. Support work here is engineering work, not a separate function.
* Translates messy customer and field requirements into architecture, including for security\\-conscious enterprise and government\\-facing buyers who care deeply about how the system isolates, logs, and proves what it did.



  





**What You Will Actually Do** 




  




* Take ownership of one or more core subsystems (orchestration, the proxy layer, credential vending, or multi\\-tenant isolation) and be the person the team routes questions to about it.
* Run point on customer\\-reported technical issues: reproduce, diagnose, fix, and communicate. Turn recurring issues into platform improvements so they stop recurring.
* Ship infrastructure on AWS using IaC, and contribute to on\\-prem / Kubernetes deployments for customers with data\\-residency or air\\-gap requirements.
* Harden the auth and compliance surface as we move through enterprise security reviews.
* Write the internal and external technical documentation that lets customers, auditors, and teammates trust how the system works.



  





**What We're Looking For** 





 We don't expect you to check every box. If you're strong in most of these and a fast learner on the rest, we want to talk.
 




  





**Core** 




  




* Solid production experience in TypeScript and/or Python. Comfortable shipping and operating real systems, and happy to grow into the other if you mainly know one today.
* Good working knowledge of AWS: compute (ECS/Fargate, Lambda), data stores (DynamoDB, relational/Postgres), and infrastructure\\-as\\-code (CDK or similar). Kubernetes experience is a bonus, not a requirement.
* A feel for systems and networking. You're curious about what's actually happening when traffic moves between services, and willing to dig into proxies, TLS, and isolation boundaries even if they're new to you.
* A security\\-aware mindset, or the instinct to develop one. Thinking about blast radius, least privilege, and "what could this credential do if it leaked."
* Comfort talking to customers. You can take a bug report, ask good questions, and own a problem through to resolution.
* Someone who learns and adapts quickly and does their best work when handed a real problem and trusted to solve it.



  





**Nice to have** 




  




* Experience with multi\\-tenant SaaS isolation models.
* Hands\\-on with SOC 2 Type 2 or similar compliance from the engineering side.
* Background in penetration testing, offensive security, or building tooling for security teams.
* Experience with enterprise SSO (e.g. Auth0\\) and identity plumbing.
* Experience deploying into regulated, government, or air\\-gapped environments.



  





**How We Work** 




  




* Small team, wide ownership, short path from idea to production.
* Engineers own their systems and the customer relationships that come with them, with plenty of support from the rest of the team.
* Direct, low\\-ceremony communication. We prefer shipped code and good decisions over status theater.
* We give our engineers real tools and real budget. We have a generous token budget for AI tooling, so you'll never be rationing the thing that makes you faster.
* The work is interesting and the problems are real. The threat model matters, the customers are sharp, and the platform has to be trustworthy enough to hand an autonomous agent the keys.



  





**Why It's Worth It** 




  






 You'd be one of the people defining how an entire category of product, autonomous offensive security, gets built safely. The surface area is large, the autonomy is real, and your work shows up directly in whether customers trust us with their infrastructure. If you want to grow fast and own something that matters, this is a good place to do it.
 




  






 Shinobi Security is an equal opportunity employer. We hire for capability and judgment.

**Responsibilities:**
 As a Software Engineer, you will join the team responsible for delivering the next generation high\\- volume, fault\\-tolerant, and scalable transaction processing system for Blackhawk. The candidate will possess a demonstrated proficiency in a wide range of skills and experience utilizing Java, NodeJS, Vue JS, Application service Interfaces, SQL, Postgres and database technologies. The desired candidate will have an excellent understanding and hands\\-on experience designing and building software components that can handle high volume traffic. Apply now for a chance to lead our products and platforms to new heights of functionality and adoption.

* Actively participate in code reviews, analyse results and choose the best solution
* Manage deadlines and prioritize tasks
* Continuously focus on improving the software quality and performance
* Works closely with the Quality Assurance team to ensure high\\-quality deliverables
* Participate in Information Security analysis and remediation
* Troubleshoot customer issues and communicate effectively to internal and external stakeholders


**Qualifications:**
* Bachelor's degree in computer science or similar field of study
* 2\\-3 years of hands‑on professional experience with object\\-oriented and functional software engineering using Java, NodeJS and related technologies
* Experience in creating, maintaining and integrating APIs
* Experience working on back\\-end services (Rest Web services, middleware)
* Experience working with Relational Database – SQL, Postgres
* Experience working in a cloud environment, preferably Amazon Web Services
* Experience in designing and building REST services and familiarity with Microservice architectures

 We seek candidates who not only demonstrate curiosity and adaptability in emerging technologies but have also successfully implemented and utilized AI tools to enhance their work, improve processes, or deliver measurable results. Our teams embrace continuous learning and the thoughtful integration of AI to create meaningful impact – for our employees and the future of work.

title: Full-Stack Software Engineer
description:
About PropertyFlow

 We're a small, founder\\-led team that ships fast. You'd own real product surface area from day one, not sit in a backlog queue. As we move parts of our stack in\\-house and launch new products, we need an engineer who's comfortable across the whole stack and happy to wear several hats.

 **build and deliver**

 What You'll Do

 **core PMS platform**

 **live website** 
 and
 **soon\\-to\\-launch mobile app**

 **pipeline**

* Work directly with the founder to scope, prioritise, and ship — translating business needs into clean, maintainable software.
* Keep security and GDPR compliance front of mind, since we handle guest IDs, payment data, and owner financials.


 What We're Looking For
 
**Core (must\\-have)** 



* Strong full\\-stack experience: a modern JavaScript/TypeScript frontend (
 **React** 
 ) plus backend API development (Node.js or similar).
* Mobile app development —
 **React Native or Flutter** 
 — with at least one app you've shipped to the App Store / Play Store.

 **Azure**

**Strong plus**

* Familiarity with the PropTech / short\\-term rental space (Channex, PriceLabs, or comparable tools).
* Experience building booking engines, calendars, or availability/pricing sync logic.
* CI/CD, automated testing, and basic DevOps.
* An eye for UI/UX — you can take a feature from sketch to polished interface without heavy hand\\-holding.
* Awareness of data protection / GDPR in a product context.



**About you** 



* You're a self\\-starter who thrives with ownership and ambiguity rather than detailed tickets.
* You're a finisher — you take products across the line and deliver, not just start them.
* You communicate clearly and work iteratively — happy to ship a working draft and refine.
* You care about the user experience and the business outcome, not just the code.


 Our Stack

 React (web), React Native / Flutter (mobile)
* **Backend:** 
 Node.js / TypeScript, REST APIs
* **Infrastructure:** 
 Microsoft Azure
* **Integrations:** 
 Channex, PriceLabs, 50\\+ OTAs, payment providers
* **Tooling:** 
 Google Workspace, Git
* **Compliance:** 
 GDPR / ICO registered, with data\\-protection processes in place


 What We Offer

* Direct collaboration with the founder and meaningful influence over product direction.
* A fast, low\\-bureaucracy environment where your work ships quickly.
* Flexible / hybrid working.
* The chance to grow with an early\\-stage company as it scales.

title: Full-Stack Developer (LMS and App Development)
description:
About Nimaya
 
 Co\\-founded by Navya Nanda and Samyak Chakrabarty, Nimaya is a future\\-ready not\\-for\\-profit that empowers young women from socio\\-economic barriers to thrive in the future of work. We are on a mission to ensure our naaris have an edge in leveraging AI at work instead of being at risk of being replaced by it.
 



 Through our flagship programs, GenAI Ready Naari and the Future Ready Naari Fellowship, we equip our learners with 21st\\-century skills, real\\-world exposure, and the confidence to pursue meaningful career pathways. To date, 10,000\\+ Nimaya Naaris across 5 states have benefited from our interventions, supported by partnerships with leading institutions including the Government of India's Yuva AI Mission.
 



  




 Why this role matters
 
 For many of our naaris, the technology they touch at Nimaya is their first real experience of a modern digital product. The learning platform and mobile app you build will be how thousands of first\\-generation learners discover AI, complete real\\-world projects, track their growth, and step into aspirational careers.
 



 This is not a back\\-office build. It is core infrastructure for the future of work for the women we serve, and it has to be as thoughtful, fast, and well\\-crafted as anything coming out of a top product company.
 



  




 What you will build and own
 * Design, build, and maintain Nimaya's Learning Management System (LMS) end to end, covering learner onboarding, content delivery, assessments, progress tracking, and reporting.
* Develop and ship a learner\\-facing mobile app built for low\\-bandwidth, mobile\\-first, Hinglish\\-friendly access.
* Build reliable backend services and APIs that power both the web platform and the app.
* Develop responsive, performant web interfaces for learners, trainers, and the internal team.
* Integrate the tools our programs run on, for example WhatsApp Business API and notification workflows, automation pipelines, Google Workspace, and analytics.
* Partner closely with the program and operations teams to turn on\\-ground learning needs into clean, usable product.
* Write and maintain tests across the stack (unit, integration, and end\\-to\\-end) so features ship reliably and stay stable as the product grows.
* Own quality across the stack: debug end to end, catch issues before learners do, and keep the platform stable as new cohorts scale.



  




 What we are looking for
 * Minimum 4 years of professional full\\-stack development experience, with strong hands\\-on work in Next.js, Node.js, and React Native.
* Solid full\\-stack fundamentals: REST APIs, databases, authentication, state management, and deployment.
* Hands\\-on experience writing and running tests (unit, integration, end\\-to\\-end) with tools such as Jest, React Testing Library, Cypress, or Playwright, and a habit of testing your own work before it ships.
* Proven ability to take a product from idea to shipped feature, not just isolated tickets.
* An eye for clean, intuitive UX, especially for first\\-time or low\\-exposure users.



  





**Good to have** 



* Experience building or working with an LMS, ed\\-tech, or learning product.
* Familiarity with integrations like WhatsApp Business API, webhooks, automation tools (n8n or similar), and Google Apps Script.
* Experience optimising for low\\-bandwidth and mobile\\-first contexts.
* Comfort working in a lean, fast\\-moving team where you wear multiple hats.



  




 Who you are
 
 You want to build for impact, at speed, and at a high bar of craft. You care about the people on the other side of the screen and want your work to reach learners who rarely get access to well\\-built technology. You are comfortable with ambiguity, you ship, and you iterate. You see this not as a social\\-sector compromise but as a chance to build a genuinely modern product for a genuinely important mission.
 



  




 How to apply
 
 To apply, please share following at "nilendu@nimaya.in":
 


* Your resume.
* **Links to live products you have built or shipped** 
 (live web apps, app store or Play Store listings, or GitHub repositories). Applications without live product links will not be considered.
